launch.granularity: one warp (32 threads) per world; one dense contact system per launch tile

global_reads.name: world_C
global_reads.notes: Full Delassus matrix stored globally, then staged as a packed lower triangle in shared memory. Scenario bytes: g1_flat 4096 B, h1_tabletop 65536 B.

global_reads.name: world_diag
global_reads.notes: Diagonal used as the PGS denominator.

global_reads.name: world_impulses
global_reads.notes: Warm-start values loaded before the sweep.

global_reads.name: world_row_type/world_row_parent/world_row_mu
global_reads.notes: Projection metadata for contact, friction, and joint-limit rows.

global_writes.name: world_impulses
global_writes.notes: Final impulses written back once after all iterations.

shared_allocations.name: s_Ctri
shared_allocations.notes: Packed lower triangle of C; this is the dominant shared-memory footprint.

shared_allocations.name: s_lam/s_rhs/s_diag
shared_allocations.notes: Impulse state and scalar row data resident across the full GS sweep.

shared_allocations.name: s_rtype/s_parent/s_mu
shared_allocations.notes: Per-row projection metadata staged once per world.

register_resident.name: my_sum/dot_sum/w_val/delta/new_impulse
register_resident.notes: Lane-local accumulators for the current row update.

streamed_inputs: The kernel streams the full dense Delassus matrix C from global memory during the load phase.
world_constraint_count gates how much of the staged shared state is actually used.
preloaded_inputs: C is repacked into s_Ctri once, then reused for every PGS iteration.
rhs, diag, impulses, and row metadata are loaded into shared memory before the solve loop.
recomputed_values: Each row recomputes the full dot product sum_j C_ij * lambda_j from shared memory every sweep.
Friction sibling projection recomputes tangential magnitude from the updated shared impulses.
dominant_work: For each active row i, evaluate w_i = rhs_i + sum_j C_ij * lambda_j.
Project lambda_i for unilateral contact / joint limit rows and the paired friction rows.
Store only the final impulse vector; no velocity vector is updated in this kernel.

observations: The dense-row kernel removes repeated global reads of C during the sweep by staging the lower triangle in shared memory, but it still requires the full C allocation to exist beforehand.
In the review scenarios the explicit C allocation is modest for g1_flat (4096 B) but already 65,536 B for h1_tabletop at M=128, before counting J/Y construction and Delassus assembly.
h1_tabletop dense-row keeps all 117 active rows in the dense system; there is no free-rigid off-ramp in this mode.
